title: Zeroization docs omit VAULT_TOKEN — token not zeroed in VaultClient
description: The module-level Zeroization section documents that secret bytes linger because vaultrs uses plain String. It does not mention that the VAULT_TOKEN itself is also not zeroed. from_uri wraps the token in Zeroizing<String>, but then calls build_vault_client(token.as_str()) which passes the token by &str to VaultClientSettingsBuilder, which copies it into a plain String inside VaultClient. The Zeroizing wrapper is dropped after from_uri returns, but the copy inside VaultClient lives for the lifetime of the backend. A user reading the docs who cares about auth-token residency in memory will not see this gap.
